21. Recognize the various roles, responsibilities, and opportunities available throughout the public, private, and nonproﬁt sectors.

22. Implement a strategy to enhance long-term career development.

REQUIREMENTS

Notice of Addendum: requirements for this program were updated effective Spring 2026 and have been published on ADDENDA TO THIS CATALOG (https://academiccatalog.umd.edu/undergraduate/ addenda/#global_health).

In order to graduate with a Bachelor of Science degree in Global Health, students must complete 120 credits, 77-83 of which are speciﬁc to the Global Health major. Students must earn a minimum grade of C- or higher in all Global Health major-required coursework.

Additional guidance for the world language and experiential learning requirements is available on the program website at https:// sph.umd.edu/gbhl-bs (https://sph.umd.edu/gbhl-bs/).

Students will need to have math eligibility of MATH120 (https:// academiccatalog.umd.edu/search/?P=MATH120) or higher to complete the Global Health Supporting Courses.
